reasoning.typography: Plain monospace terminal aesthetic with zero typographic refinement — feels like raw markdown/CLI output, not a designed type system
reasoning.hierarchy: Weak visual hierarchy — markdown headings (#, ##, ###) provide structure but no bold visual dominance; everything feels equally weighted in the monotone
reasoning.distinctiveness: Radically distinctive — a 'machine version' that literally documents an AI agent signup bonus and reads like API documentation. No one else is doing this.
reasoning.color: Limited monochrome palette (black/white/gray) but not because of discipline — it's the absence of color design. Links appear standard blue, breaking the system.
reasoning.density: Strong content density with confident information packing — reads like documentation/spec, not afraid of text blocks and bullet lists without decorative padding
reasoning.voice: Exceptional voice-structure fit — the 'Machine Version' conceit perfectly executed as literal plain-text documentation for AI consumption. IA reflects genuine utility (promotional offer upfront, then product details)

winning_patterns: Brazenly honest 'machine version' concept — literally a page designed for AI agents to parse
Information-first density without apology — every line has utility
Use-case bullets that actually describe real scenarios instead of marketing fluff
Novel IA: promotional offer at top (smart for AI agent context), then technical product breakdown
anti_patterns: Zero typographic craft — looks like unformatted terminal output
No visual hierarchy whatsoever — flat gray text soup
Standard blue hyperlinks break the monochrome system
While conceptually bold, execution is just... markdown. No design labor visible.
_note: Big numbers, photography, bold full-bleed strips
